feed_baseline: Roughly 40 other low/medium-risk Identity Protection 'unfamiliar sign-in properties' / 'atypical travel' flags across the tenant this week, from legitimately traveling or remote/WFH staff using new devices, hotel/airport Wi-Fi, or VPN exit nodes -- ordinary interactive sign-ins with risk Low/Medium, no follow-on activity.

Normal interactive sign-in volume for the ~150-account tenant (staff, IT/admin, service accounts, and a handful of external/guest/contractor accounts), business-hours pattern, mostly from corporate IP ranges and known devices.

The tenant's ~15 active app registrations/service principals as a whole -- besides the 5 named benign integrations and the two apps tied to the incident/pentest, a handful of unremarkable internal tools (Teams add-ins, calendar-sync utilities) with narrow scopes and no mail/file access.
Routine mail flow for ~150 Exchange Online mailboxes; the shared receipts@corp.com mailbox receiving routine forwarded receipts from Finance staff.
Ordinary business-hours SharePoint Online activity across ~30 sites (Finance Reporting, HR, departmental sites, Project Atlas) at normal/low volume.

Routine change tickets and vendor/engagement records on itsm01 (e.g. the CHG tickets for the benign apps, the IT-2025-0734 MFA-exception record, the PT-2026-03 engagement record) -- list-level visibility only, not the full record contents.
Routine dc01/on-prem AD activity and Entra Connect sync cycles every 30 minutes, reflecting on-prem account changes into the tenant.
Normal outbound traffic from fw01/on-prem hosts to common SaaS/cloud destinations -- nothing related to the incident traverses this on-prem firewall.
Clean EDR/endpoint telemetry on ws01 (jdoe's admin workstation) -- normal day-to-day M365/Entra ID administration activity, no malware or unusual processes.
